channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Roy Ayers,'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' musician, passes away at 84
item.description: Los Angeles native, Roy Ayers, who was behind the popular song "Everybody Loves the Sunshine" has passed away. He was 84 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pamela Bach, actor and ex-wife of David Hasselhoff, dies at 62
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Pamela Bach, an actor and the ex-wife of “Baywatch” star David Hasselhoff, has died at 62. The Los Angeles medical examiner's office reports that she died Wednesday and the cause is still under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Facing Trump's threats, Columbia investigates students critical of Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University senior Maryam Alwan was visiting family in Jordan over winter break when she received an email from the school accusing her of discriminatory harassment. Her supposed top offense: writing an op-ed in the student newspaper calling for divestment from Israel.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missing man misidentified, buried in veterans cemetery
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- The West Tennessee State Veterans Cemetery is being asked to exhume a body after a DNA report revealed a man buried five years ago was misidentified as an Army veteran who is still alive.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Job cuts last month higher than any February since 2009
item.description: U.S.-based employers cut more jobs last month than in any February in 16 years, according to a new report from outplacement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect’s parents are no-shows for accused killer son's court appearance
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his brother and a family cat allegedly told police he had a "fit of madness" when they responded to the scene of the crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers and government officials in a broad cybercrime campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Twelve Chinese nationals — including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company — have been charged in connection with global cybercrime campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and DAKE KANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger's defense claims he has autism in bid to avoid firing squad
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's autism diagnosis should take the death penalty off the table if he is convicted of the Idaho student murders, defense lawyer argues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denver mayor grilled over area’s Tren de Aragua problem as GOP lawmaker says policies to blame
item.description: Democrat Denver Mayor Mike Johnston is taking heat from state lawmakers over "weak immigration laws" and faced Congress over sanctuary status this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has dropped a high-profile abortion case in Idaho. Here's what that means
item.description: A yearslong legal battle over the right to an emergency abortion in Idaho has been abruptly upended now that President Donald Trump has moved to drop the high-profile case.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi And Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Avalanche buries 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska, officials say
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and likely killed under the weight of dozens of feet of snow, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday. If the deaths are confirmed, it would be the deadliest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trudeau not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves some tariffs on Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is unwilling to lift Canada’s retaliatory tariffs on the United States if President Donald Trump leaves any U.S. tariffs on Canada, a senior government official told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ launches investigation into UC system over antisemitism allegations
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department announced on Wednesday that it has launched a civil investigation into the University of California system under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act. Under Title VII, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US travel advisory for Turks and Caicos Islands updated, year after Americans arrested
item.description: The updated advisory comes nearly a year after several Americans were arrested on ammunition charges in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Marines and soldiers discharged for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine show limited interest in rejoining
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Roughly 650 Marines and Army soldiers who were forced out of the military for refusing to get the COVID-19 vaccine have so far expressed interest in returning to the force under an executive order from President Donald Trump, officials said this week.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican introduces censure resolution against Al Green
item.description: The House will vote this week on a resolution from Rep. Dan Newhouse (R-Wash.) to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his outburst at President Trump’s speech to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate face Florida criminal probe days after US homecoming
item.description: The Tate brothers are facing an 'active' criminal investigation in Florida after flying there via private jet amid a human trafficking case in Romania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Driver surrenders to police after pursuit full of PIT maneuvers, standoffs
item.description: A Los Angeles Police pursuit in the San Fernando Valley was full of starts and stops Tuesday afternoon, but the driver eventually surrendered to police.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ash Wednesday: What you should know about the start of the season
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) – What is Ash Wednesday? Ash Wednesday 2025, which falls on March 5 this year, marks the official beginning of the Christian season of Lent, which culminates with Easter on April 20, 2025. The 46-day season is meant for penitence, sacrifice, and reflection.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, Fox 5
